title: Hyderabad sees intermittent showers after midnight rain, IMD forecasts more thundershowers
summary: At 10.49 a.m., IMD issued an alert forecasting light to moderate thundershowers for the next few hours in the northern parts of the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC).

title: Government mandates airlines to reserve 60% of seats free of charge
summary: The Ministry, in its latest release, urges strict adherence to ‘passenger rights framework, particularly in cases of delays, cancellations and denied boarding’

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: DMK signs poll deal with CPI, allots five seats
summary: Mr. Veerapandian said the DMK front was an alliance shaped by principles

title: Parliament Budget Session LIVE: PM Modi, LoP Kharge bid farewell to retiring Rajya Sabha MPs on completion of their terms
summary: The Lok Sabha began its proceedings for the day with Question Hour

title: Watch: Why is Maharashtra’s Freedom of Religion Bill so controversial?
summary: Maharashtra’s Freedom of Religion Bill, 2026 has sparked a major political and legal debate. The proposed law aims to prevent forced religious conversions through strict rules, including a 60-day prior notice and severe penalties. While the government says it protects vulnerable individuals, critics argue it could violate fundamental rights, enable misuse, and impact personal freedom and interfaith relationships. The bill has been passed in both houses.

title: Three MBBS students killed in scooter accident in Kerala’s Kozhikode
summary: The accident took place around 2.30 a.m. when the electric scooter they were travelling on crashed into an electric post and fell into a drainage

title: Eight killed in Indore house fire after EV charging point blast; probe ordered
summary: There was an explosion at the charging point, and the fire in the car then engulfed the house, says Police Commissioner Santosh Kumar Singh

title: LPG crunch pushes PM POSHAN kitchens back to firewood, burdening women workers
summary: The PM POSHAN scheme which serves about 11 crore children across 10.35 lakh schools, mostly from socially disadvantaged and low-income backgrounds, is now reverting to alternative fuels
